rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation scope
Preserve the AADL architecture by maintaining the original component structure and connections
Do not modify the AADL model structure; only insert AGREE annex blocks in valid scopes
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks are syntactically correct and valid in the target scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use valid AGREE syntax for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Ensure that the inserted AGREE annex blocks are syntactically correct and valid in the target scope
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in the AGREE annex blocks
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not modify the AADL model structure; only insert AGREE annex blocks in valid scopes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for the properties
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
